evidence_quotes.quote: I mainly view video games as a product, something which I buy once and should be able to access even when I have no internet access.
If the game is free to access and play as part of a multiplayer experience, I would understand the possibility of it shutting down in the future.
But nowadays we are not even left with a guarantee or a warning of when a product might shut down and be unavailable to access anymore.

But if I purchase it, I fully expect and believe that I have a right to use it, modify it, and share it ( if possible like sharing a CD or game-cartridge physically to another person ) years into the future.

I also believe that consumers should have the right, option and access to upkeep a server privately or for public use for games ( product ) where the distributors no longer claim that they have the means to upkeep a multiplayer experience.

If a distributor / developer ceases the development of a game and moves on to a different project, I also believe that owners of a copy of the digital ( or physical ) product, should be able to access it completely and again modify it or do with it as they wish, barring any intent of copyright infringement or other malicious intents other than keeping a product alive and available for use.

I consider games to be a product, and if I purchase it once as opposed to renting it, I expect to keep it forever and have the options to upkeep the experience as I see fit no matter what happens to the original distributor of the product Stop killing games

paragraphs.text: I mainly view video games as a product, something which I buy once and should be able to access even when I have no internet access. If the game is free to access and play as part of a multiplayer experience, I would understand the possibility of it shutting down in the future. But nowadays we are not even left with a guarantee or a warning of when a product might shut down and be unavailable to access anymore. If I were to rent a product, I would understand the limited time I have to access said product. But if I purchase it, I fully expect and believe that I have a right to use it, modify it, and share it ( if possible like sharing a CD or game-cartridge physically to another person ) years into the future. I also believe that consumers should have the right, option and access to upkeep a server privately or for public use for games ( product ) where the distributors no longer claim that they have the means to upkeep a multiplayer experience. If a distributor / developer ceases the development of a game and moves on to a different project, I also believe that owners of a copy of the digital ( or physical ) product, should be able to access it completely and again modify it or do with it as they wish, barring any intent of copyright infringement or other malicious intents other than keeping a product alive and available for use. I consider games to be a product, and if I purchase it once as opposed to renting it, I expect to keep it forever and have the options to upkeep the experience as I see fit no matter what happens to the original distributor of the product Stop killing games
